reviews.review_msg: His lectures were very long, and a night class, but he cares so much about his students that it almost forgives that. His lectures are quite dry, but the material is really interesting, and he gives you so much feedback, plus, he's a decent marker. He is a nice person, and honestly wants his student's input and response!

Whatley loves to use the full four hours for lectures. The lectures are really boring and the only really teaches you two concepts in 13 weeks. He is really nice and understanding and gives realy good feedback. Only two essays and a final. He also chooses really good books to read but the lectures are really dry.

John was passionate, brilliant, gave great feedback, open about the final exam, was approachable and available to help,wanted us to do well. The only things is the lectures were dense, but he included group discussions too. Lots of readings, but I actually learned a lot about theory. Overall he is a great teacher who wants you to succeed.
terrible class, boring and dry material. The prof sits there to read through his slides. Basically makes you suffer in all ways.

There were a lot of readings for the course, but they were all relevant to the course content.  John clearly lays out material and explains the concepts thoroughly; ask questions as they are encouraged and some of the material is complex and dense.  Of all the English courses I've taken (I've taken a fair few), John is the best prof I've had.

Our TA had some sort of personal crisis plus computer trouble and didn't bother to get someone to cover him.  The prof was MIA and didn't seem to care that the TA wasn't doing his job, despite many of us trying to contact him for support.  Worst educational experience of my adult life.
Useless is right, someone said arrogant and I think that is kind, thinks he is MR CRIM - he's NOT! Take anything else, if Brockman or Davies are teaching take a course with them!!!

Honestly the nicest prof ever! Super helpful, open to opinions on literature, and very approachable. Extremely lenient on crim students who do not have English Lit backgrounds. Made me love Dostoevsky! Would take him a class with him again in a heartbeat
Pompous! Sets meetings and then doesn't show. Two hours on the bus twice and both times he didn't show. Condescending when trying to arrange the third attempt.

Apart from his extremely sooth-me-to-sleep voice, he's a relatively good prof. Basically creates a great group discussion, but he does require that everyone talk at some point or another, but no embarrasing pointing fingers if you didn't do the reading. Highly recommended.
